methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
BurnBot, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Anukool Lakhina that has raised $25M to date, was valued through triangulation against a universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA). The analysis applied two distinct methodologies.

Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a valuation proxy, applied a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $50M. Peer-median analysis examined three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—producing a median valuation of $150M and a range of $110M to $200M. These two methods reflect material divergence, likely driven by differences in capital efficiency and maturity within the Series A cohort.

The triangulated valuation range spans $42.5M (low) to $172.5M (high), with a median of $100M. The stage-progression method produces the conservative floor, while peer multiples suggest substantially higher valuation is supportable in current market conditions for this sector. The key uncertainty remains capital intensity and time-to-revenue relative to the three direct peers; if BurnBot's unit economics or capital requirements diverge meaningfully from Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy, positioning within this range should shift accordingly. Additional diligence on burn rate and pathway to profitability would refine the upper bound of this range.
